addressed: HiveEngineSpec.where_latest_partition() now validates partition column names against a strict identifier regex before passing them to sqlalchemy.Column(), preventing SQL injection via crafted partition column names in database metadata
structured_output.reasoning: Partition column names retrieved from database metadata (via database.get_indexes() → latest_partition()) were passed directly to sqlalchemy.Column() without validation. In multi-tenant environments, unprivileged users who create tables can influence partition column metadata. The fix adds a regex check (^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$) that rejects any column name containing SQL injection characters (semicolons, spaces, quotes, etc.) while allowing all legitimate identifier names. Invalid names are logged and skipped gracefully.
